_comment: TZ-90 Phase A audit artifact. Light + dark theme getComputedStyle() expectations after the 5 CSS edits to frontend/src/styles.css shipped 2026-07-11. Computed values reflect what the CSS rules produce when correctly parsed by modern Chromium/Firefox/Safari. Cross-check with live browser via DevTools on /overlays to confirm parity.

backdrop._previous_value_round5: rgba(0,0,0,0.3) (30% editorial; replaced per TZ-90 user-spec)

animation._phase_b_dependency: Phase A ships keyframe + class; Phase B wires .pi-dialog-host-open via PiDialogService.open()
animation._scope_check: <head> must contain `@keyframes pi-dialog-scale-in` for the animation to be applicable; verifiable via document.styleSheets inspection

panel._note: var(--dialog-radius) overridden only by light; same 8px in dark

backdrop._note: var(--overlay-bg) override only by light; same 0.5 in dark

animation._phase_b_dependency: Same as light — animation applied per open() call

TZ-DIALOG-OVERFLOW-FIX-rounds-1-5: @media print reset retained + ADDED box-shadow: none !important for paper rendering
TZ-DIALOG-VISIBILITY-FIX-round-5: background-color: oklch-from-var-primary path RETAINED via --overlay-bg token alias
RGB fallback RETAINED via --overlay-bg-fallback token alias

phase_b_dependency_note: Phase A only SHIPS the @keyframes + .pi-dialog-host-open CSS. Phase B (TZ-90B) adds `variant` + `width=xl` to <app-pi-dialog>, and PiDialogService.open() will add .pi-dialog-host-open class to the dialog host element after attach(). Until Phase B runs, opening a dialog will NOT trigger the animation (no class binding yet). Browser-verification of animation should be deferred until B ships.
_verification_path: Run live browser check on http://localhost:4200/overlays → open Section IV 'Алерты' button → run the probe commands → compare to this file's expected values for both themes. Mark verified_locally=true + add date when parity confirmed.
next_step_user_action: Open http://localhost:4200/overlays in Chrome DevTools, toggle dark mode via localStorage('pi.theme','dark') + html.dark, open any dialog from Section IV, and run the probe commands in the console. Expected values should match this JSON byte-for-byte (within decimal rounding).
